processed_reviews.original_text:
The staff here were very friendly, professional, and courteous, which made for a welcoming check-in. The room itself was an adequate size and the beds were somewhat comfortable for the stay.

However, the bathroom really needs the most attention. There is no vanity storage space, there was visible mold in the caulk line, and the tub stopper was completely broken. Worst of all, the shower pressure was absolutely abysmal it felt like a weak drizzle of rain rather than an actual shower. Great service from the team, but the bathroom maintenance needs a major upgrade.
processed_reviews.cleaned_text: The staff here were very friendly, professional, and courteous, which made for a welcoming check-in. The room itself was an adequate size and the beds were somewhat comfortable for the stay. However, the bathroom really needs the most attention. There is no vanity storage space, there was visible mold in the caulk line, and the tub stopper was completely broken. Worst of all, the shower pressure was absolutely abysmal it felt like a weak drizzle of rain rather than an actual shower. Great service from the team, but the bathroom maintenance needs a major upgrade.

processed_reviews.original_text: The fact that customers could not control room temperature made our stay a little uncomfortable. We had to sleep in layers. When we asked if our room temperature was malfunctioning we were told they controlled it and could only be changed if they received enough complaints.

processed_reviews.original_text: This hotel is located in a safe area of Mid-town and walking distance to many NYC restaurants and sites.  The staff is friendly and the hotel has a small, intimate feeling when you stay.  We have stayed here twice and will book again in the future.  Housekeeping and concierge staff are fantastic!  Special Thank you to Evelyn, Nancy and Pedro!
processed_reviews.cleaned_text: This hotel is located in a safe area of Mid-town and walking distance to many NYC restaurants and sites. The staff is friendly and the hotel has a small, intimate feeling when you stay. We have stayed here twice and will book again in the future. Housekeeping and concierge staff are fantastic! Special Thank you to Evelyn, Nancy and Pedro!

processed_reviews.original_text: I highly recommend this hotel for your next stay in New York City.  The hotel is conveniently located to many city attractions, as well as excellent restaurants and shopping.  The part is located across the street from Bryant Park and the New York City Public Library.  It is not very far from Central Park and many other attractions.  The area is very clean and we felt very comfortable there.  The hotel is clean and the staff is very helpful and friendly.  My family and I feel that it provided us great value for the price that we paid.

My family and I feel that it provided us great value for the price that we paid.

processed_reviews.original_text: Everything with the stay went fine, except we were thinking we'd get a room with queen beds as advertised.

processed_reviews.original_text: If you're looking for small overpriced rooms that scream 1987 this is your place. Carpet in room made me keep a walk around pair of socks next to my bed just to use restroom or walk around. The decor looked like it was hand-me-downs. Bathroom and toilet were even more outdated than the rest of the room. The toilet had that 80's yellowish color to it. Bed sheets look like they came balled up out of a plastic bag. Wall paper on walls looked like it was hanging on for dear life.

Bathroom and toilet were even more outdated than the rest of the room. The toilet had that 80's yellowish color to it. Bed sheets look like they came balled up out of a plastic bag. Wall paper on walls looked like it was hanging on for dear life.

processed_reviews.original_text: I was nervous about driving into the City for this stay, but getting to the hotel, and even parking around the corner, was relatively easy. It's also an easy walk from Grand Central. We loved being close to the library and Bryant Park. The hotel was pleasantly quiet considering how close it is to everything. Our room was simple and clean. Two fast elevators and an efficient front desk kept the lobby from being crowded. Hotel staff were incredibly helpful - very kind and patient. It was amazing to be able to leave our bags there securely until later in the day after we checked out.

Our room was simple and clean. Two fast elevators and an efficient front desk kept the lobby from being crowded. Hotel staff were incredibly helpful - very kind and patient. It was amazing to be able to leave our bags there securely until later in the day after we checked out.

processed_reviews.original_text: I took a red eye from California and requested for eqlry checkin so i could be at my business meetings ready and in time. Hotel faculty didn't honor the request and didnt bother to ask about how they can help solve for the needs. They said they will call as soon as a room would be available but never called. I ahd yo show up to ask for a room again. Disrupted my entire day and made it invain to lodge in downtown closer to my meeting venue.

processed_reviews.original_text: This hotel is a huge step below any Marriott property I’ve stayed in across the US, Europe, Canada, Mexico, etc. Courtyard has always been my safe go-to for business travel, but there is no way I could ever stay here again. Super rude and unhelpful staff - like going out of their way to be rude to my employees who were asking normal hotel/travel related questions. Overall a baffling experience. Why do you work in hospitality if you hate people?

processed_reviews.original_text:
The room was not very clean, and it was extremely hot. I reported the issue, but was told that since it’s April, the hotel is still running the heater. There was no real attempt to fix the problem, even though I was traveling with my kids.
Very disappointing experience. I would not stay there again and would not recommend this hotel. It felt like they didn’t care about their guests.

It felt like they didn t care about their guests.

processed_reviews.original_text:
My 15 year old son and I spent a few days in Manhattan for spring break. This hotel worked out well for us: we each had our own bed, we had a nice view of the city skyline, we were close to food and Bryant Park, and it was pretty clean. I like that the TV had a menu where you could sign into Netflix or HBO, etc. 
There was a lot of construction noise on the street below, which is not the hotel's fault. We slept fine, despite the noise. The beds were firm.

processed_reviews.original_text: Overall, location was fine and close to meetings I needed to attend in the area. Room was dated and carpet was really dirty and just made the space feel dirty.
